1	# Plan 001: Build the Stage 0 observed job-review runtime
     2	
     3	> **Executor instructions**: Read this entire plan before editing. Follow each
     4	> step in order. Run every verification command and confirm the expected result
     5	> before continuing. If a STOP condition occurs, stop and report. Do not invent
     6	> a workaround.
     7	>
     8	> **Required writer model**: The only writer must be `deepseek-v4-pro`, running
     9	> inside a fresh isolated git worktree. Do not substitute another model or use a
    10	> fallback. Three fresh `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions review the same candidate
    11	> commit independently after implementation. The review protocol is defined
    12	> below.
    13	>
    14	> **Baseline warning**: This plan was written at commit `5423187` while the main
    15	> checkout had a large intentional dirty worktree. The required `discussion/`
    16	> and `diagram/` inputs were untracked. A worktree created from `5423187` will
    17	> not contain them. Do not execute this plan until the intended cleanup, design
    18	> notes, fixtures, and diagrams are committed to the canonical branch. Then run
    19	> the reconcile step in `plans/README.md`, replace the planned-at SHA below,
    20	> refresh every current-state excerpt, and rerun the drift check.
    21	>
    22	> **Drift check after baseline reconciliation**:
    23	>
    24	> ```bash
    25	> git diff --stat <reconciled-base-sha>..HEAD -- \
    26	>   pyproject.toml uv.lock src/haxjobs/cli.py \
    27	>   src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
    28	>   src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces \
    29	>   tests discussion/fixtures/harness diagram docs/implementation-reports
    30	> git status --short -- \
    31	>   pyproject.toml uv.lock src/haxjobs/cli.py \
    32	>   src/haxjobs/model src/haxjobs/agent_core \
    33	>   src/haxjobs/employment src/haxjobs/interfaces \
    34	>   tests discussion/fixtures/harness diagram docs/implementation-reports
    35	> ```
    36	>
    37	> Expected before editing: no unowned changes in any in-scope path. If an
    38	> excerpt below differs from live code or discussion decisions, stop and ask
    39	> for the plan to be refreshed.
    40	
    41	## Status
    42	
    43	- **Priority**: P1
    44	- **Effort**: L
    45	- **Risk**: MED
    46	- **Depends on**: none, but blocked by baseline and model-availability gates
    47	- **Category**: direction, architecture, tests, DX
    48	- **Planned at**: commit `5423187`, 2026-07-17, against an intentionally dirty working tree
    49	
    50	## Why this matters
    51	
    52	HaxJobs currently has an older single-turn client and tool loop, but the new product is greenfield. The decided build method starts smaller: one truthful career fixture, one real job fixture, one provider call, one saved trace, and one human review.
    53	
    54	This plan creates that Stage 0 floor using the Pi-inspired separation selected for the new runtime:
    55	
    56	```text
    57	model boundary -> agent core -> employment layer -> interface
    58	```
    59	
    60	The outcome is not a generic agent framework. It is one manually runnable job-review experiment that can be repeated against Job 49 and Job 328 without editing code.
    61	
    62	## Decided product contract
    63	
    64	The executor must preserve these decisions from `discussion/`:
    65	
    66	- Hax is the conversational employment agent. HaxJobs is the system around it.
    67	- The immediate task is: "What do you think of this role? Is it worth my time?"
    68	- Job 49 runs first as the evidence-rich control case.
    69	- Job 328 runs second as the missing-evidence stress case.
    70	- The career fixture is a frozen backend-career slice derived from the user's real evidence.
    71	- Stage 0 exposes no tools and makes exactly one model request.
    72	- Python owns the runtime. Pydantic v2 validates trust-boundary contracts.
    73	- `uv`, pytest, local redacted JSONL, Python logging, and Markdown human review form the first verification floor.
    74	- There is one configured provider adapter and one scripted fake. No provider fallback.
    75	- The current web app, database, product actions, sessions, compaction, skills, sub-agents, and monitoring stay out.
    76	- The new runtime mirrors Pi's conceptual boundaries inside one Python package.
    77	- Current code may only be reused when it independently fits the new design.
    78	- Do not add compatibility wrappers around the old agent path.
    79	
    80	## Current state
    81	
    82	### Repository condition
    83	
    84	At planning time:
    85	
    86	- HEAD was `5423187` on `main`.
    87	- The worktree had hundreds of intentional cleanup deletions and untracked design files.
    88	- `tests/` did not exist in the working tree.
    89	- Python compilation passed.
    90	- `uv lock --check` passed.
    91	- pytest failed before collection with `ERROR: file or directory not found: tests/`.
    92	- `drawio` was available at `/usr/bin/drawio`.
    93	- Pydantic `2.13.4` and OpenAI `2.44.0` were installed, but Pydantic was not a direct project dependency.
    94	
    95	The dirty state is why execution must wait for a committed baseline.
    96	
    97	### Existing model path is legacy input, not the new architecture
    98	
    99	`src/haxjobs/agent/agent.py` currently combines provider configuration, provider calls, raw provider messages, and tool-loop control in one class:
   100	
   101	```python
   102	class Agent:
   103	    def run(self, prompt: str, system: str | None = None, ...) -> str:
   104	        ...
   105	
   106	    def run_with_tools(self, prompt: str, system: str | None = None, ...) -> str:
   107	        ...
   108	```
   109	
   110	Do not refactor this path in Plan 001. It has old callers in the current product. The new experiment must not import `haxjobs.agent`.
   111	
   112	Temporary coexistence is explicit. It is not a compatibility layer. A later cleanup plan may delete the legacy path after the new runtime proves itself.
   113	
   114	### Existing CLI is the installed interface seam
   115	
   116	`pyproject.toml` already exposes:
   117	
   118	```toml
   119	[project.scripts]
   120	haxjobs = "haxjobs.cli:main"
   121	```
   122	
   123	`src/haxjobs/cli.py` uses `argparse`. Add one thin experiment subcommand there. The CLI must delegate to `haxjobs.interfaces.experiment_cli`; it must not assemble prompts or call providers itself.
   124	
   125	### Provider credentials
   126	
   127	The current local credential file is `~/.haxjobs/haxjobs.toml`. It contains private provider configuration and must never be copied, printed, traced, committed, or exposed to review agents.
   128	
   129	The new model boundary may independently reuse this small file contract because it fits the configured-provider requirement. It must parse it through a new typed model-layer loader rather than importing a web setup service.
   130	
   131	Environment fallback may support the existing headless variables, but the adapter must never log values.
   132	
   133	### Fixtures
   134	
   135	`discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md` is the source for the two tracked job fixtures.
   136	
   137	Job 49 must preserve:
   138	
   139	- Trainline IT Support Analyst
   140	- direct Ashby source identity and URL
   141	- the stored job description, including its known truncation
   142	- IT support responsibilities and requirements
   143	- no old score, verdict, recommended CV, or unsupported sponsorship conclusion
   144	
   145	Job 328 must preserve only what the source record actually contains:
   146	
   147	- Oritain Software Engineer, Mid-Level, Full Stack title
   148	- LinkedIn source URL
   149	- London location as stored
   150	- the 149-character title-and-URL description stub
   151	- no old Django, FastAPI, TypeScript, cloud, company-mission, or sponsorship claims
   152	
   153	## Target architecture after this plan
   154	
   155	```text
   156	haxjobs.model
   157	  provider-neutral request/response contracts
   158	  configured OpenAI-compatible adapter
   159	  scripted fake adapter
   160	
   161	haxjobs.agent_core
   162	  internal messages
   163	  one-call Stage 0 runner
   164	  safe event and artifact writing
   165	  structured run result
   166	
   167	haxjobs.employment
   168	  career and job fixture contracts
   169	  Hax stable instructions
   170	  job-review flow instructions
   171	  source-labelled context assembly
   172	  run_job_review action
   173	
   174	haxjobs.interfaces
   175	  argparse-facing experiment adapter
   176	
   177	haxjobs.cli
   178	  thin command registration only
   179	```
   180	
   181	The package names are intentionally different from the legacy `haxjobs.agent` path.
   182	
   183	## Commands you will need
   184	
   185	Run from the repository root.
   186	
   187	| Purpose | Command | Expected on success |
   188	|---|---|---|
   189	| Lock check | `uv lock --check` | exit 0 |
   190	| Focused tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model tests/agent_core tests/employment tests/interfaces` | all pass |
   191	| Full surviving tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/` | all pass |
   192	| Compile | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')` | exit 0 |
   193	| Cron syntax | `bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh` | exit 0 |
   194	| CLI help | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help` | help text, exit 0 |
   195	| Diff check | `git diff --check` | no output |
   196	| XML check | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio')"` | exit 0 |
   197	| Draw.io export | `drawio --export --format png --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage0-check.png diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio` | exit 0 and non-empty PNG |
   198	
   199	Do not run frontend commands if the reconciled baseline intentionally removed `frontend/`. If it exists at the reconciled base, run its documented typecheck, lint, and build commands too.
   200	
   201	## Suggested executor toolkit
   202	
   203	Read before coding:
   204	
   205	- `discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md`
   206	- `discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md`
   207	- `discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md`
   208	- `discussion/research/2026-07-17-pi-hermes-job-native-harness-study.md`
   209	- `docs/harness-primitives/00-How-An-Agent-Actually-Runs.md`
   210	- `.agents/skills/readable-code/SKILL.md`
   211	- `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md`
   212	
   213	Use the clean-drawio skill for the required diagram. Do not create Mermaid as a substitute.
   214	
   215	## Scope
   216	
   217	### In scope
   218	
   219	Existing files:
   220	
   221	- `pyproject.toml`
   222	- `uv.lock`
   223	- `src/haxjobs/cli.py`
   224	- `diagram/README.md`
   225	
   226	New tracked paths:
   227	
   228	- `src/haxjobs/model/__init__.py`
   229	- `src/haxjobs/model/contracts.py`
   230	- `src/haxjobs/model/config.py`
   231	- `src/haxjobs/model/openai_compatible.py`
   232	- `src/haxjobs/model/fake.py`
   233	- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/__init__.py`
   234	- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/messages.py`
   235	- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/events.py`
   236	- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/result.py`
   237	- `src/haxjobs/agent_core/runner.py`
   238	- `src/haxjobs/employment/__init__.py`
   239	- `src/haxjobs/employment/fixtures.py`
   240	- `src/haxjobs/employment/instructions.py`
   241	- `src/haxjobs/employment/job_review.py`
   242	- `src/haxjobs/interfaces/__init__.py`
   243	- `src/haxjobs/interfaces/experiment_cli.py`
   244	- `discussion/fixtures/harness/job-49.json`
   245	- `discussion/fixtures/harness/job-328.json`
   246	- `discussion/fixtures/harness/backend-career.example.json`
   247	- `discussion/fixtures/harness/job-review-rubric.md`
   248	...

    74	- There is one configured provider adapter and one scripted fake. No provider fallback.
    75	- The current web app, database, product actions, sessions, compaction, skills, sub-agents, and monitoring stay out.
    76	- The new runtime mirrors Pi's conceptual boundaries inside one Python package.
    77	- Current code may only be reused when it independently fits the new design.
    78	- Do not add compatibility wrappers around the old agent path.
    79	
    80	## Current state
    81	
    82	### Repository condition
    83	
    84	At planning time:
    85	
    86	- HEAD was `5423187` on `main`.
    87	- The worktree had hundreds of intentional cleanup deletions and untracked design files.
    88	- `tests/` did not exist in the working tree.
    89	- Python compilation passed.
    90	- `uv lock --check` passed.
    91	- pytest failed before collection with `ERROR: file or directory not found: tests/`.
    92	- `drawio` was available at `/usr/bin/drawio`.
    93	- Pydantic `2.13.4` and OpenAI `2.44.0` were installed, but Pydantic was not a direct project dependency.
    94	
    95	The dirty state is why execution must wait for a committed baseline.
    96	
    97	### Existing model path is legacy input, not the new architecture
    98	
    99	`src/haxjobs/agent/agent.py` currently combines provider configuration, provider calls, raw provider messages, and tool-loop control in one class:
   100	
   101	```python
   102	class Agent:
   103	    def run(self, prompt: str, system: str | None = None, ...) -> str:
   104	        ...
   105	
   106	    def run_with_tools(self, prompt: str, system: str | None = None, ...) -> str:
   107	        ...
   108	```
   109	
   110	Do not refactor this path in Plan 001. It has old callers in the current product. The new experiment must not import `haxjobs.agent`.
   111	
   112	Temporary coexistence is explicit. It is not a compatibility layer. A later cleanup plan may delete the legacy path after the new runtime proves itself.
   113	
   114	### Existing CLI is the installed interface seam
   115	
   116	`pyproject.toml` already exposes:
   117	
   118	```toml
   119	[project.scripts]
   120	haxjobs = "haxjobs.cli:main"
   121	```
   122	
   123	`src/haxjobs/cli.py` uses `argparse`. Add one thin experiment subcommand there. The CLI must delegate to `haxjobs.interfaces.experiment_cli`; it must not assemble prompts or call providers itself.
   124	
   125	### Provider credentials
   126	
   127	The current local credential file is `~/.haxjobs/haxjobs.toml`. It contains private provider configuration and must never be copied, printed, traced, committed, or exposed to review agents.
   128	
   129	The new model boundary may independently reuse this small file contract because it fits the configured-provider requirement. It must parse it through a new typed model-layer loader rather than importing a web setup service.
   130	
   131	Environment fallback may support the existing headless variables, but the adapter must never log values.
   132	
   133	### Fixtures
   134	
   135	`discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md` is the source for the two tracked job fixtures.
   136	
   137	Job 49 must preserve:
   138	
   139	- Trainline IT Support Analyst
   140	- direct Ashby source identity and URL
   141	- the stored job description, including its known truncation
   142	- IT support responsibilities and requirements
   143	- no old score, verdict, recommended CV, or unsupported sponsorship conclusion
   144	
   145	Job 328 must preserve only what the source record actually contains:
   146	
   147	- Oritain Software Engineer, Mid-Level, Full Stack title
   148	- LinkedIn source URL
   149	- London location as stored
   150	- the 149-character title-and-URL description stub
   151	- no old Django, FastAPI, TypeScript, cloud, company-mission, or sponsorship claims
   152	
   153	## Target architecture after this plan
   154	
   155	```text
   156	haxjobs.model
   157	  provider-neutral request/response contracts
   158	  configured OpenAI-compatible adapter
   159	  scripted fake adapter
   160	
   161	haxjobs.agent_core
   162	  internal messages
   163	  one-call Stage 0 runner
   164	  safe event and artifact writing
   165	  structured run result
   166	
   167	haxjobs.employment
   168	  career and job fixture contracts
   169	  Hax stable instructions
   170	  job-review flow instructions
   171	  source-labelled context assembly
   172	  run_job_review action
   173	
   174	haxjobs.interfaces
   175	  argparse-facing experiment adapter
   176	
   177	haxjobs.cli
   178	  thin command registration only
   179	```
   180	
   181	The package names are intentionally different from the legacy `haxjobs.agent` path.
   182	
   183	## Commands you will need
   184	
   185	Run from the repository root.
   186	
   187	| Purpose | Command | Expected on success |
   188	|---|---|---|
   189	| Lock check | `uv lock --check` | exit 0 |
   190	| Focused tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model tests/agent_core tests/employment tests/interfaces` | all pass |
   191	| Full surviving tests | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/` | all pass |
   192	| Compile | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')` | exit 0 |
   193	| Cron syntax | `bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh` | exit 0 |
   194	| CLI help | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help` | help text, exit 0 |
   195	| Diff check | `git diff --check` | no output |
   196	| XML check | `PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio')"` | exit 0 |
   197	| Draw.io export | `drawio --export --format png --output /tmp/haxjobs-stage0-check.png diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio` | exit 0 and non-empty PNG |
   198	
   199	Do not run frontend commands if the reconciled baseline intentionally removed `frontend/`. If it exists at the reconciled base, run its documented typecheck, lint, and build commands too.

301	- browser automation
   302	
   303	## Git workflow
   304	
   305	- Worktree branch after baseline reconciliation: `exec/001-stage0-observed-job-review-<run-id>`.
   306	- The DeepSeek v4 Pro writer is the only writer in that worktree.
   307	- Commit logical units with the repo's plain commit style, for example: `Build Stage 0 observed job review`.
   308	- Do not push, merge, or cherry-pick automatically.
   309	- Never run `git stash`, `git reset`, `git clean`, or `git add -A` in the user's checkout.
   310	- Stage explicit paths only.
   311	- The candidate worktree must finish with no staged or unstaged tracked changes.
   312	
   313	## Execution and review protocol
   314	
   315	### Gate 1: committed baseline
   316	
   317	Before creating the worktree:
   318	
   319	1. Confirm the required discussion and diagram files exist at the reconciled commit.
   320	2. Confirm `git status --short` is clean in the canonical checkout, or that any remaining dirt is explicitly disjoint and approved.
   321	3. Record the base SHA, branch, and a SHA-256 of `git status --porcelain=v2 -z --untracked-files=all`.
   322	4. Do not copy uncommitted files into the executor worktree.
   323	
   324	### Gate 2: exact model availability
   325	
   326	The orchestration runtime must prove these exact models are available without fallback:
   327	
   328	| Role | Model | Count | Access |
   329	|---|---|---:|---|
   330	| Writer | `deepseek-v4-pro` | 1 | write in isolated worktree |
   331	| Architecture reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
   332	| Correctness reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
   333	| Deliverables reviewer | `deepseek-v4-flash` | 1 | read-only |
   334	
   335	Use runtime metadata, not model self-report, to record the canonical model ID and session ID. Require four distinct sessions. If any exact model is unavailable, rate-limited, aliased to another model, or silently falls back, stop. Do not substitute.
   336	
   337	### Writer pass
   338	
   339	Give the DeepSeek v4 Pro writer:
   340	
   341	- this plan only
   342	- the committed repository
   343	- the listed discussion references
   344	- the explicit scope and STOP conditions
   345	- no secret values
   346	
   347	The writer implements, runs all objective checks, completes both live fixture reviews, creates the report and diagram, and commits the candidate.
   348	
   349	### Independent Flash review team
   350	
   351	Run three fresh DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers against the same candidate SHA. They are read-only and do not see each other's findings.
   352	
   353	1. **Architecture and scope reviewer**
   354	   - Checks four-layer separation.
   355	   - Checks the CLI is only an adapter.
   356	   - Checks no old agent import, tool loop, session, database, or speculative feature entered.
   357	   - Checks one request snapshot and one provider call.
   358	
   359	2. **Correctness, privacy, and tests reviewer**
   360	   - Checks Pydantic boundaries.
   361	   - Checks no network in tests.
   362	   - Checks trace redaction and file permissions.
   363	   - Checks provider errors are sanitized.
   364	   - Checks fixtures contain no old evaluation or invented evidence.
   365	
   366	3. **Deliverables, manual run, and diagram reviewer**
   367	   - Runs the manual CLI with the fake provider.
   368	   - Verifies live-run receipts without reading private fixture contents.
   369	   - Verifies the Markdown report against actual diff and command results.
   370	   - Parses and exports the Draw.io file, then checks the PNG.
   371	
   372	Each returns `approve`, `changes-required`, or `blocked`, with file and line evidence.
   373	
   374	Any blocker or high finding returns to the same Pro writer. The writer fixes and commits. Rerun all checks. Then launch three new Flash sessions against the new candidate SHA. Final acceptance requires three approvals on the same SHA. Stop after three fix rounds and report the unresolved issue.
   375	
   376	## Steps
   377	
   378	### Step 0: Reconcile the baseline before execution
   379	
   380	This is an operator/advisor step, not implementation work.
   381	
   382	- Commit the intended cleanup, `discussion/`, `diagram/`, and other source-worthy changes outside Improve execution.
   383	- Run `improve reconcile` so this plan receives the new SHA and refreshed excerpts.
   384	- Confirm `tests/` is intentionally absent or present at that baseline.
   385	- Confirm the old frontend and dev script status.
   386	
   387	**Verify**:
   388	
   389	```bash
   390	git status --short
   391	git rev-parse --short HEAD
   392	git show HEAD:discussion/004-minimal-job-native-harness.md >/dev/null
   393	git show HEAD:discussion/005-implementation-stack-observability-and-verification.md >/dev/null
   394	git show HEAD:discussion/006-pi-inspired-haxjobs-architecture.md >/dev/null
   395	```
   396	
   397	Expected: clean or explicitly approved disjoint status, one reconciled SHA, and all three design notes available from Git.
   398	
   399	### Step 1: Freeze machine-readable fixtures
   400	
   401	Create the two tracked job JSON files from `discussion/fixtures/003-five-job-sample.md`.
   402	
   403	Use a Pydantic fixture contract with fields for:
   404	
   405	- fixture ID and version
   406	- job reference
   407	- source type
   408	- source URL
   409	- title
   410	- company identity as actually known
   411	- location as actually known
   412	- description snapshot
   413	- observation timestamp or date
   414	- source completeness
   415	- known truncation
   416	- explicit unknowns
   417	
   418	Do not include old evaluations, scores, fit levels, recommended CV variants, old model prose, or unsupported sponsorship risk.
   419	
   420	Create `backend-career.example.json` as a schema-shaped example containing fake placeholders only.
   421	
   422	Create the real local fixture at `state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json` from the user's current profile and evidence. Keep only:
   423	
   424	- preferred name
   425	- one backend career direction
   426	- target roles and seniority
   427	- location and work-mode constraints relevant to the task
   428	- sponsorship requirement as a user constraint
   429	- selected evidence claims with source type, evidence strength, and opaque private source references
   430	
   431	Exclude contact details, document paths, credential material, exact immigration document identifiers, and unrelated career tracks. The user must review this fixture before live runs.
   432	
   433	Create the human rubric from the Job 49 and Job 328 checks in `discussion/004`.
   434	
   435	**Verify**:
   436	
   437	```bash
   438	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/employment/test_job_review.py -k fixture
   439	python3 - <<'PY'
   440	import json
   441	from pathlib import Path
   442	for path in [
   443	    Path('discussion/fixtures/harness/job-49.json'),
   444	    Path('discussion/fixtures/harness/job-328.json'),
   445	    Path('discussion/fixtures/harness/backend-career.example.json'),
   446	]:
   447	    json.loads(path.read_text())
   448	    print(path)
   449	PY
   450	```
   451	
   452	Expected: valid JSON, fixture tests pass, and Job 328 remains a title-and-URL stub.
   453	
   454	### Step 2: Add the provider-neutral model boundary
   455	
   456	Add Pydantic as a direct dependency in `pyproject.toml` and update `uv.lock`.
   457	
   458	Define only the Stage 0 contracts:
   459	
   460	- `ModelMessage`
   461	- `ModelRequest`
   462	- `ModelResponse`
   463	- `TokenUsage`
   464	- async `ModelClient` protocol
   465	- typed provider configuration
   466	
   467	Do not add tool-call models yet.
   468	
   469	Use `AsyncOpenAI` inside the OpenAI-compatible adapter. Project provider messages there. Keep provider-specific request options inside the adapter.
   470	
   471	The configuration loader may read `~/.haxjobs/haxjobs.toml` and documented environment fallbacks. It must validate required fields and return sanitized errors. Never include an API key in `repr`, logs, exceptions, traces, or reports.
   472	
   473	The fake adapter accepts a scripted sequence of responses and records received normalized requests. It makes no network calls.
   474	
   475	**Verify**:
   476	
   477	```bash
   478	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/model/test_model_boundary.py
   479	uv lock --check
   480	```
   481	
   482	Expected: all model-boundary tests pass and lock check exits 0.
   483	
   484	### Step 3: Build internal messages and the one-call agent core
   485	
   486	Keep internal messages separate from provider messages. Stage 0 needs user, system, and assistant text. Do not model tool messages yet.
   487	
   488	Create `RunResult` with:
   489	
   490	- schema version
   491	- run ID
   492	- final text
   493	- exit reason
   494	- exact provider and model IDs
   495	- one turn count
   496	- usage when available
   497	- sanitized error when present
   498	- artifact directory
   499	
   500	The runner must:
   501	
   502	1. freeze the request snapshot
   503	2. create a private run directory with mode `0700`
   504	3. emit `run_started`
   505	4. emit `context_prepared` with IDs and hashes only
   506	5. emit `model_started`
   507	6. call the model exactly once
   508	7. emit `model_completed` or sanitized `model_failed`
   509	8. write the normalized result
   510	9. emit `run_completed` or `run_failed`
   511	10. return `RunResult`
   512	
   513	Write files with mode `0600`:
   514	
   515	- `events.jsonl`: redacted stable events
   516	- `manifest.json`: versions and hashes
   517	- `transcript.json`: exact normalized messages and final text, local only
   518	- `result.json`: structured result
   519	- `review.md`: copied human rubric template
   520	
   521	The JSONL writer is a passive observer. If it fails, preserve the model result and record a warning through Python logging where possible.
   522	
   523	Never store the provider raw payload, headers, API key, or full provider configuration.
   524	
   525	**Verify**:
   526	
   527	```bash
   528	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/agent_core/test_stage0_runner.py
   529	```
   530	
   531	Expected: event order, one-call enforcement, redaction, permissions, success, provider failure, and passive-observer failure tests all pass.
   532	
   533	### Step 4: Build the employment job-review action
   534	
   535	Create one `run_job_review(...)` function in `haxjobs.employment.job_review`.
   536	
   537	It must:
   538	
   539	1. validate the career and job fixtures
   540	2. build the stable Hax instruction block from `discussion/004`
   541	3. build the job-review flow block
   542	4. assemble four source-labelled blocks in this order:
   543	   - user request
   544	   - career direction and constraints
   545	   - selected user evidence
   546	   - job source snapshot
   547	5. expose an empty active-tool list in the manifest
   548	6. call the Stage 0 runner
   549	7. return the structured result
   550	
   551	Do not include current time inside the stable prompt. Put volatile observation dates inside labelled context. Keep prompt versions explicit.
   552	
   553	Do not include the old evaluation data or unrelated profile fields.
   554	
   555	**Verify**:
   556	
   557	```bash
   558	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/employment/test_job_review.py
   559	```
   560	
   561	Expected: ordering, source labels, hidden-evaluation exclusion, stable prompt version, manifest hashes, and no-tool context tests pass.
   562	
   563	### Step 5: Add the minimal CLI interface
   564	
   565	Add an `experiment` command to `src/haxjobs/cli.py`. The only logic there should be parser registration and delegation.
   566	
   567	Implement the command in `haxjobs.interfaces.experiment_cli`:
   568	
   569	```text
   570	haxjobs experiment review-job \
   571	  --job job-49 \
   572	  --caree...

   781	
   782	### `tests/model/test_model_boundary.py`
   783	
   784	Cover:
   785	
   786	- typed config validation without exposing secret values
   787	- normalized text response
   788	- usage absent and present
   789	- provider stop reason
   790	- scripted fake order
   791	- fake request capture
   792	- sanitized provider error
   793	- no network use
   794	
   795	### `tests/agent_core/test_stage0_runner.py`
   796	
   797	Cover:
   798	
   799	- exact event order
   800	- exactly one model call
   801	- frozen request content
   802	- empty active-tool manifest
   803	- success result
   804	- provider failure result
   805	- passive observer failure does not change model result
   806	- redaction before JSONL write
   807	- private run-directory and file permissions
   808	- raw provider payload absent
   809	
   810	### `tests/employment/test_job_review.py`
   811	
   812	Cover:
   813	
   814	- Job 49 fixture contains the real IT-support evidence
   815	- Job 328 remains a title-and-URL stub
   816	- old evaluation fields cannot validate as fixture input
   817	- career fixture example has no contact fields
   818	- context block order and source labels
   819	- stable and flow instructions are separate
   820	- active tools are empty
   821	- old evaluation prose never enters context
   822	
   823	### `tests/interfaces/test_experiment_cli.py`
   824	
   825	Cover:
   826	
   827	- help output
   828	- fake provider smoke run
   829	- unsupported job ref
   830	- missing career fixture
   831	- invalid fixture
   832	- configured provider failure returns non-zero and sanitized text
   833	- CLI delegates to the employment action
   834	
   835	Do not snapshot full natural-language answers.
   836	
   837	## Done criteria
   838	
   839	All must hold:
   840	
   841	- [ ] The plan was reconciled to a clean committed baseline after `5423187`.
   842	- [ ] One DeepSeek v4 Pro writer implemented the candidate in an isolated worktree.
   843	- [ ] No fallback or substitute writer model was used.
   844	- [ ] The four new Python layers exist and do not import `haxjobs.agent`.
   845	- [ ] Pydantic v2 is a direct dependency and `uv lock --check` passes.
   846	- [ ] Job 49 and Job 328 tracked fixtures exclude old evaluations.
   847	- [ ] The owner-reviewed real career fixture exists only under ignored `state/`.
   848	- [ ] Stage 0 makes exactly one provider request.
   849	- [ ] Stage 0 exposes zero tools and emits zero tool events.
   850	- [ ] Fake-provider tests use no network.
   851	- [ ] All focused and surviving full tests pass.
   852	- [ ] Python compilation and cron shell syntax pass.
   853	- [ ] The manual fake command runs without source edits.
   854	- [ ] Configured-provider Job 49 and Job 328 runs complete.
   855	- [ ] Both human rubric files are complete and accepted.
   856	- [ ] Local artifacts use restrictive permissions.
   857	- [ ] No private fixture, raw trace, answer, credential, or runtime artifact is tracked.
   858	- [ ] `diagram/003-stage-0-observed-job-review.drawio` parses and exports.
   859	- [ ] The tracked PNG matches the current Draw.io source by fresh export and visual review.
   860	- [ ] `diagram/README.md` indexes the current-state diagram.
   861	- [ ] The delivery report contains actual evidence and a manual run guide.
   862	- [ ] Three independent DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers approve the same candidate SHA.
   863	- [ ] No out-of-scope file changed.
   864	- [ ] Candidate worktree has no staged or unstaged tracked changes after commit.
   865	- [ ] The source checkout status fingerprint is unchanged by execution.
   866	
   867	## STOP conditions
   868	
   869	Stop and report instead of improvising if:
   870	
   871	- the intended dirty cleanup and discussion state has not been committed
   872	- any required discussion file is absent from the execution base
   873	- any in-scope file has unowned changes
   874	- `deepseek-v4-pro` or three independent `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions are unavailable without fallback
   875	- the runtime cannot prove canonical model IDs through metadata
   876	- the real career fixture is missing, unsupported, or not owner-reviewed
   877	- the configured provider is unavailable
   878	- a unit test would need live network access
   879	- a trace or report contains a credential or unnecessary private career content
   880	- the implementation begins importing or rewriting `src/haxjobs/agent/`
   881	- Stage 0 requires a tool, database, session, browser, framework, or workflow engine
   882	- Job 49 cannot identify the broad IT-support mismatch after one explainable revision
   883	- Job 328 does not expose insufficient evidence or source inspection as the next useful check
   8...

1	# HaxJobs greenfield implementation plans
     2	
     3	Generated on 2026-07-17 from the decided material in `discussion/` and the Pi/Hermes source study.
     4	
     5	This is the first build wave for the new HaxJobs runtime. It deliberately plans only Stage 0 and Stage 1:
     6	
     7	```text
     8	Stage 0
     9	  one observed provider call, no tools
    10	
    11	Stage 1
    12	  the same runner, one inspect_job_source(job_ref) tool
    13	```
    14	
    15	Stage 2 conversation history, Stage 3 saved decisions, sessions, career memory, company watching, coding workspaces, and every broader product capability wait for evidence from these runs.
    16	
    17	## Current execution blockers
    18	
    19	The plans are written, but execution must not start yet.
    20	
    21	### 1. The canonical baseline is not committed
    22	
    23	At planning time:
    24	
    25	- HEAD was `5423187` on `main`.
    26	- The worktree contained a large intentional cleanup.
    27	- Required `discussion/` and `diagram/` files were untracked.
    28	- `tests/` was deleted in the working tree.
    29	- Several old agent files had uncommitted edits.
    30	
    31	An isolated worktree created from `5423187` would not contain the design state these plans depend on.
    32	
    33	Required action before execution:
    34	
    35	1. Commit the intended cleanup, discussion notes, fixtures, diagrams, and source-worthy files through the normal developer workflow.
    36	2. Do not ask Improve execution to commit or merge them.
    37	3. Run `improve reconcile` after the clean commit.
    38	4. Replace each plan's planned-at SHA with the new baseline.
    39	5. Refresh current-state excerpts and in-scope drift checks.
    40	
    41	### 2. Exact DeepSeek execution models are not currently proved
    42	
    43	The inspected subagent runtime listed GPT-based built-ins. It did not expose runtime mappings for:
    44	
    45	- `deepseek-v4-pro`
    46	- `deepseek-v4-flash`
    47	
    48	The user requires DeepSeek v4 Pro as the sole implementation writer and a team of DeepSeek v4 Flash reviewers. Before execution, the runtime must prove the exact canonical model IDs through metadata without fallback.
    49	
    50	Do not substitute Codex, Claude, another DeepSeek alias, or a fallback model.
    51	
    52	## Execution order and status
    53	
    54	| Plan | Title | Priority | Effort | Depends on | Status |
    55	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    56	| [001](001-build-stage-0-observed-job-review.md) | Build the Stage 0 observed job-review runtime | P1 | L | committed baseline, exact DeepSeek models | BLOCKED |
    57	| [002](002-build-stage-1-source-tool-loop.md) | Build the Stage 1 bounded source-inspection loop | P1 | L | accepted Plan 001 and Job 328 evidence gate | BLOCKED |
    58	
    59	Status values:
    60	
    61	- `TODO`
    62	- `IN PROGRESS`
    63	- `DONE`
    64	- `BLOCKED: <reason>`
    65	- `REJECTED: <reason>`
    66	
    67	## Dependency graph
    68	
    69	```text
    70	commit current greenfield design baseline
    71	        |
    72	        v
    73	prove deepseek-v4-pro and deepseek-v4-flash availability
    74	        |
    75	        v
    76	Plan 001: Stage 0
    77	  Job 49 -> Job 328 -> human reviews -> delivery report
    78	        |
    79	        | only if Job 328 proves source inspection is needed
    80	        v
    81	Plan 002: Stage 1
    82	  same runner + one source tool -> comparison report
    83	        |
    84	        v
    85	stop and choose the next plan from observed failures
    86	```
    87	
    88	Plan 002 must not begin because Plan 001 tests passed alone. Its report and completed Job 328 human rubric must show that source inspection is the next missing capability.
    89	
    90	## Architecture boundary for this wave
    91	
    92	The current user instruction resolves the implementation shape for Stage 0 and Stage 1:
    93	
    94	```text
    95	Model boundary
    96	  provider-neutral contracts, configured adapter, fake adapter
    97	
    98	Agent core
    99	  messages, bounded loop, events, results
   100	
   101	Employment layer
   102	  Hax instructions, fixtures, context, one employment action
   103	
   104	Interface
   105	  a thin argparse CLI
   106	```
   107	
   108	Keep these as modules inside one Python package. No package monorepo.
   109	
   110	The old `src/haxjobs/agent/` path remains out of scope during this wave. New code must not import it or wrap it. Temporary coexistence is recorded honestly. Deletion or migration needs a later explicit plan after the new runtime works.
   111	
   112	## Required execution team for every plan
   113	
   114	### Writer
   115	
   116	- One `deepseek-v4-pro` session.
   117	- Sole writer in a fresh isolated worktree.
   118	- Receives the plan, committed repository, and named discussion references.
   119	- Must not receive secret values.
   120	- Implements, tests, runs fixtures, writes the delivery report, creates the diagram, and commits the candidate.
   121	
   122	### Independent review team
   123	
   124	Three fresh `deepseek-v4-flash` sessions inspect the same candidate SHA without seeing each other's findings:
   125	
   126	1. Architecture and scope reviewer.
   127	2. Correctness, privacy, safety, and tests reviewer.
   128	3. Deliverables, manual CLI, evidence report, and Draw.io reviewer.
   129	
   130	Reviewers are read-only. They return:
   131	
   132	- `approve`
   133	- `changes-required`
   134	- `blocked`
   135	
   136	The same Pro writer fixes accepted findings. All checks rerun. Three new Flash sessions review the replacement SHA. Final acceptance requires three approvals on the same candidate SHA.
   137	
   138	Stop after three failed fix rounds.
   139	
   140	## Deliverables required from every plan
   141	
   142	Every plan must finish with all of these:
   143	
   144	| Deliverable | Plan 001 | Plan 002 |
   145	|---|---|---|
   146	| Manually runnable CLI | `haxjobs experiment review-job` Stage 0 | same command with Stage 1 |
   147	| Deterministic fake-model tests | required | extended |
   148	| Configured-provider fixture runs | Job 49 and Job 328 | Job 49 and Job 328 |
   149	| Local redacted JSONL traces | required | required |
   150	| Completed human rubrics | two | two |
   151	| Tracked delivery report | `docs/implementation-reports/001-...md` | `docs/implementation-reports/002-...md` |
   152	| Current-state Draw.io | `diagram/003-...drawio` | `diagram/004-...drawio` |
   153	| PNG exported from Draw.io | required | required |
   154	| Diagram index update | required | required |
   155	| Three Flash approvals | required | required |
   156	
   157	The Markdown delivery report is the durable evidence. Raw prompts, model answers, fetched source text, private career fixtures, credentials, and provider payloads stay local and untracked.
   158	
   159	## Manual interface progression
   160	
   161	After Plan 001:
   162	
   163	```bash
   164	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
   165	  --job job-49 \
   166	  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
   167	  --provider fake \
   168	  --output-dir state/harness-runs
   169	```
   170	
   171	Use `--provider configured` for the real provider.
   172	
   173	After Plan 002:
   174	
   175	```bash
   176	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
   177	  --job job-328 \
   178	  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
   179	  --stage 1 \
   180	  --max-turns 3 \
   181	  --provider configured \
   182	  --output-dir state/harness-runs
   183	```
   184	
   185	The CLI is an experiment interface. It does not become the final career CLI in this wave.
   186	
   187	## Verification baseline
   188	
   189	Every plan runs:
   190	
   191	```bash
   192	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/
   193	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')
   194	bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh
   195	uv lock --check
   196	git diff --check
   197	```
   198	
   199	If the reconciled baseline still contains a frontend, also run its documented typecheck, lint, and build commands. If the cleanup intentionally removed it, do not recreate it merely to satisfy an old command.
   200	
   201	Tests added by these plans must not call live models or the public network.
   202	
   203	Configured-provider and source-inspection runs are separate manual evidence steps.
   204	
   205	## Draw.io rule for every plan
   206	
   207	Use `.agents/skills/clean-drawio/SKILL.md`.
   208	
   209	Each diagram must:
   210	
   211	- show the code that exists at the accepted candidate SHA
   212	- use grouped swimlanes
   213	- keep to five to seven groups
   214	- stay under roughly 35 cells
   215	- keep file paths out of nodes
   216	- use thick orthogonal inter-group arrows
   217	- include geometry for every edge
   218	- parse as XML
   219	- export through the real local Draw.io command
   220	- have a tracked PNG generated from the same source
   221	- be indexed in `diagram/README.md`
   222	- link to the plan's implementation report for file-level detail
   223	
   224	A design-only future component must not appear as if implemented.
   225	
   226	## Evidence report rule for every plan
   227	
   228	Each tracked report must record:
   229	
   230	- base and candidate SHAs
   231	- exact writer and reviewer runtime model identities
   232	- changed paths grouped by architecture layer
   233	- tests added
   234	- actual commands and exit codes
   235	- manual run commands
   236	- safe run IDs and artifact hashes
   237	- human rubric verdicts
   238	- diagram counts and export evidence
   239	- reviewer findings and resolution
   240	- known limits
   241	- next evidence gate
   242	
   243	Never record:
   244	
   245	- secret values
   246	- provider configuration values
   247	- full prompts
   248	- complete model answers
   249	- CV or profile text
   250	- full fetched pages
   251	- authorization headers
   252	- cookies
   253	- raw provider payloads
   254	
   255	## Why later plans are not written yet
   256	
   257	The discussion explicitly chose an evidence-driven staircase:
   258	
   259	```text
   260	run a real job
   261	-> inspect the trace
   262	-> name the exact failure
   263	-> add one missing capability
   264	-> rerun the same job
   265	```
   266	
   267	Writing plans now for sessions, compaction, career graphs, watches, project workspaces, or sub-agents would ignore that decision and recreate the earlier architecture drift.
   268	
   269	After Plan 002, read its report and choose one next plan from observed evidence. Possible outcomes include:
   270	
   271	- conversational correction if follow-up handling is the next failure
   272	- one saved decision if the user-response handoff is the next failure
   273	- source normalization if retrieval quality is the next failure
   274	- context selection if the career fixture is the next failure
   275	- no new primitive if the current result is already good enough
   276	
   277	## Considered and rejected for this wave
   278	
   279	- **Refactor the existing `src/haxjobs/agent/` loop first**: rejected because it has old callers and would mix migration with the experiment.
   280	- **Delete the old runtime in Plan 001**: rejected because that expands the first model-call experiment into a product migration.
   281	- **Use PydanticAI immediately**: rejected by the decided stack. Reconsider only after local plumbing grows enough that the framework deletes meaningful tested code.
   282	- **Add web search with source inspection**: rejected. Stage 1 measures one tool.
   283	- **Add a database for run traces**: rejected. Local JSONL and Markdown are the decided floor.
   284	- **Add OpenTelemetry or Phoenix**: rejected until local traces become hard to inspect or runs cross processes.
   285	- **Plan Stage 2 and Stage 3...

   233	- tests added
   234	- actual commands and exit codes
   235	- manual run commands
   236	- safe run IDs and artifact hashes
   237	- human rubric verdicts
   238	- diagram counts and export evidence
   239	- reviewer findings and resolution
   240	- known limits
   241	- next evidence gate
   242	
   243	Never record:
   244	
   245	- secret values
   246	- provider configuration values
   247	- full prompts
   248	- complete model answers
   249	- CV or profile text
   250	- full fetched pages
   251	- authorization headers
   252	- cookies
   253	- raw provider payloads
   254	
   255	## Why later plans are not written yet
   256	
   257	The discussion explicitly chose an evidence-driven staircase:
   258	
   259	```text
   260	run a real job
   261	-> inspect the trace
   262	-> name the exact failure
   263	-> add one missing capability
   264	-> rerun the same job
   265	```
   266	
   267	Writing plans now for sessions, compaction, career graphs, watches, project workspaces, or sub-agents would ignore that decision and recreate the earlier architecture drift.
   268	
   269	After Plan 002, read its report and choose one next plan from observed evidence. Possible outcomes include:
   270	
   271	- conversational correction if follow-up handling is the next failure
   272	- one saved decision if the user-response handoff is the next failure
   273	- source normalization if retrieval quality is the next failure
   274	- context selection if the career fixture is the next failure
   275	- no new primitive if the current result is already good enough
   276	
   277	## Considered and rejected for this wave
   278	
   279	- **Refactor the existing `src/haxjobs/agent/` loop first**: rejected because it has old callers and would mix migration with the experiment.
   280	- **Delete the old runtime in Plan 001**: rejected because that expands the first model-call experiment into a product migration.
   281	- **Use PydanticAI immediately**: rejected by the decided stack. Reconsider only after local plumbing grows enough that the framework deletes meaningful tested code.
   282	- **Add web search with source inspection**: rejected. Stage 1 measures one tool.
   283	- **Add a database for run traces**: rejected. Local JSONL and Markdown are the decided floor.
   284	- **Add OpenTelemetry or Phoenix**: rejected until local traces become hard to inspect or runs cross processes.
   285	- **Plan Stage 2 and Stage 3...

   314	
   315	### Gate 1: committed baseline
   316	
   317	Before creating the worktree:

   560

   562	
   563	### Step 5: Add the minimal CLI interface
   564	
   565	Add an `experiment` command to `src/haxjobs/cli.py`. The only logic there should be parser registration and delegation.
   566	
   567	Implement the command in `haxjobs.interfaces.experiment_cli`:
   568	
   569	```text
   570	haxjobs experiment review-job \
   571	  --job job-49 \
   572	  --career-fixture state/harness-fixtures/backend-career-v1.json \
   573	  --provider fake|configured \
   574	  --output-dir state/harness-runs
   575	```
   576	
   577	Requirements:
   578	
   579	- default provider is `fake`, so a manual smoke run costs nothing
   580	- configured provider requires explicit `--provider configured`
   581	- supported Stage 0 jobs are `job-49` and `job-328`
   582	- default fixture directory is `discussion/fixtures/harness`
   583	- print the final text, run ID, stop reason, and artifact path
   584	- do not print the assembled prompt, provider config, or raw transcript path unless the user explicitly asks for local debug details
   585	- return non-zero on validation or provider failure
   586	
   587	**Verify**:
   588	
   589	```bash
   590	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job --help
   591	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run haxjobs experiment review-job \
   592	  --job job-49 \
   593	  --career-fixture discussion/fixtures/harness/backend-career.example.json \
   594	  --provider fake \
   595	  --output-dir /tmp/haxjobs-stage0-smoke
   596	```
   597	
   598	Expected: help exits 0; smoke command exits 0 and prints a run ID plus artifact directory.
   599	
   600	### Step 6: Run deterministic verification before live calls
   601	
   602	Run the focused and full surviving test floors. Tests must not access the network.
   603	
   604	**Verify**:
   605	
   606	```bash
   607	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m pytest -q tests/
   608	PYTHONPATH=src:. uv run python3 -m py_compile $(find src tests cron -name '*.py')
   609	bash -n cron/run_pipeline.sh
   610	uv lock --check
   611	git diff --check
   612	```
   613	
   614	Expected: every command exits 0. If frontend exists at the reconciled baseline, its typecheck, lint, and build also pass.
   615	
   616	### Step 7: Run Job 49 and Job 328 against the configured provider
   617	
   618	Use the owner-reviewed local career fixture and the configured provider. Never print or copy credentials.
   619	
   620	```bash

   898	- Plan 002 may start only when the Job 328 Stage 0 report proves source inspection is the next missing capability.

text:
1	# Handoff Plan Template
     2	
     3	Every plan is written for an executor model that has **zero context**: it has not seen the advisor session, the audit, the other plans, or any prior conversation. It may be a smaller/cheaper model. Assume it is competent at following explicit instructions and weak at filling gaps, recovering from ambiguity, or knowing when to stop.
     4	
     5	Three properties make a plan executable by a weaker model:
     6	
     7	1. **Self-contained context** — everything needed is in the file: paths, code excerpts, conventions, commands.
     8	2. **Verification gates** — every step ends with a command and its expected result. The executor never has to *judge* whether it succeeded.
     9	3. **Hard boundaries and escape hatches** — explicit out-of-scope list, and "STOP and report" conditions instead of letting the model improvise when reality doesn't match the plan.
    10	
    11	File naming: `plans/NNN-short-slug.md`, numbered in recommended execution order.
    12	
    13	---
    14	
    15	## Template
    16	
    17	```markdown
    18	# Plan NNN: <Imperative title — what will be true after this plan>
    19	
    20	> **Executor instructions**: Follow this plan step by step. Run every
    21	> verification command and confirm the expected result before moving to the
    22	> next step. If anything in the "STOP conditions" section occurs, stop and
    23	> report — do not improvise. When done, update the status row for this plan
    24	> in `plans/README.md` — unless a reviewer dispatched you and told you they
    25	> maintain the index.
    26	>

     3	Every plan is written for an executor model that has **zero context**: it has not seen the advisor session, the audit, the other plans, or any prior conversation. It may be a smaller/cheaper model. Assume it is competent at following explicit instructions and weak at filling gaps, recovering from ambiguity, or knowing when to stop.

1	[project]
     2	name = "haxjobs"
     3	version = "1.0.0.dev0"
     4	description = "Self-hosted job search platform"
     5	requires-python = ">=3.12"
     6	license = {text = "MIT"}
     7	authors = [{name = "Arinze Elenasulu"}]
     8	readme = "README.md"
     9	dependencies = [
    10	    "fastapi>=0.115",
    11	    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.34",
    12	    "markdown>=3.10.2",
    13	    "openai>=2.44.0",
    14	    "python-multipart>=0.0.32",
    15	]
    16	
    17	[project.scripts]
    18	haxjobs = "haxjobs.cli:main"
    19	
    20	[build-system]
    21	requires = ["hatchling"]
    22	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
    23	
    24	[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
    25	packages = ["src/haxjobs"]
    26	
    27	[dependency-groups]
    28	dev = [
    29	    "pytest>=9.1.1",
    30	]

--- cli ---
     1	"""HaxJobs CLI."""
     2	import argparse
     3	import sys
     4	
     5	
     6	def _csv(value: str | None) -> list[str] | None:
     7	    if not value:
     8	        return None
     9	    return [item.strip() for item in value.split(",") if item.strip()]
    10	
    11	
    12	def cmd_start(args):
    13	    """Start the HaxJobs server."""
    14	    from haxjobs.server.main import run
    15	    run(host=args.host, port=args.port, open_browser=not args.no_browser, reload=args.reload)
    16	
    17	
    18	def cmd_agent_ask(args):
    19	    """Ask the native HaxJobs agent a question from the terminal."""
    20	    from haxjobs.agent import Agent, build_system_prompt, load_identity
    21	
    22	    prompt = " ".join(args.prompt).strip()
    23	    if not prompt and not sys.stdin.isatty():
    24	        prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    25	    if not prompt:
    26	        raise SystemExit("Provide a prompt or pipe one on stdin.")
    27	
    28	    system = args.system
    29	    if not args.plain:
    30	        system = build_system_prompt(load_identity(), context_files=system or "")
    31	
    32	    tools = _csv(args.tools)
    33	    if tools:
    34	        text = Agent(tools=tools).run_with_tools(
    35	            prompt,
    36	            system=system,
    37	            max_turns=args.max_turns,
    38	        )
    39	    else:
    40	        text = Agent().run(prompt, system=system)
    41	    print(text)
    42	
    43	
    44	def cmd_dev_reset(args):
    45	    """Reset onboarding state for development — keeps provider config."""
    46	    from pathlib import Path
    47	    from urllib import request
    48	    from haxjobs.config import STATE_DIR
    49	
    50	    # Clear project runtime state
    51	    removed = []
    52	    if STATE_DIR.exists():
    53	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
    54	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
    55	                continue  # ponytail: preserve DB, just clear profile
    56	            if p.is_file():
    57	                p.unlink()
    58	            elif p.is_dir():
    59	                import shutil
    60	                shutil.rmtree(p)
    61	            removed.append(p.name)
    62	    if removed:
    63	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
    64	    else:
    65	        print("State: nothing to clear")
    66	
    67	    # Also clear in-memory session if server is running
    68	    try:
    69	        req = request.Request(
    70	            f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/reset",
    71	            method="POST",
    72	        )
    73	        request.urlopen(req, timeout=2)
    74	        print("Server session: cleared")
    75	    except Exception:
    76	        try:
    77	            request.urlopen(
    78	                f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/reset",
    79	                timeout=2,
    80	            )
    81	            print("Server session: cleared")
    82	        except Exception:
    83	            print("Server session: not running (run: haxjobs dev restart)")
    84	
    85	
    86	def cmd_dev_status(args):
    87	    """Show current onboarding state."""
    88	    from pathlib import Path
    89	    from haxjobs.features.onboarding.service import load_profile
    90	
    91	    print(f"Profile file : {'exists' if load_profile() else 'none'}")
    92	
    93	    try:
    94	        from urllib import request
    95	        import json
    96	        req = request.Request(f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/status")
    97	        resp = request.urlopen(req, timeout=2)
    98	        data = json.loads(resp.read())
    99	        print(f"Server status: {data.get('stage', 'unknown')}")
   100	
   101	        resp = request.urlopen(f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/discovery/status", timeout=2)
   102	        discovery = json.loads(resp.read())
   103	        connected = isinstance(discovery.get("running"), bool) and isinstance(discovery.get("scrapers"), list)
   104	        print(f"Discovery API: {'connected' if connected else 'stale'}")
   105	    except Exception:
   106	        print("Server status: not running")
   107	
   108	
   109	def cmd_dev_restart(args):
   110	    """Kill running dev server and start a fresh one with reload."""
   111	    import os, signal, subprocess, sys
   112	    from pathlib import Path
   113	
   114	    # Kill existing
   115	    killed = False
   116	    for line in os.popen(f"ss -tlnp 'sport = :{args.port}'").readlines():
   117	        if "pid=" in line:
   118	            import re
   119	            m = re.search(r"pid=(\d+)", line)
   120	            if m:
   121	                os.kill(int(m.group(1)), signal.SIGTERM)
   122	                killed = True
   123	                print(f"Killed pid {m.group(1)}")
   124	
   125	    # Reset filesystem state (skip API call since we just killed the server)
   126	    from haxjobs.config import STATE_DIR
   127	    removed = []
   128	    if STATE_DIR.exists():
   129	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
   130	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
   131	                continue
   132	            if p.is_file():
   133	                p.unlink()
   134	            elif p.is_dir():
   135	                import shutil
   136	                shutil.rmtree(p)
   137	            removed.append(p.name)
   138	    if removed:
   139	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
   140	
   141	    # Start fresh
   142	    print("Starting dev server…")
   143	    os.chdir(args.project_root or os.getcwd())
   144	    subprocess.Popen(
   145	        [
   146	            sys.executable,
   147	            "-m",
   148	            "haxjobs.server.main",
   149	            "--no-browser",
   150	            "--reload",
   151	            "--host",
   152	            args.host,
   153	            "--port",
   154	            str(args.port),
   155	        ],
   156	        env={**os.environ, "PYTHONPATH": "src:."},
   157	        stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   158	        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   159	    )
   160	    print(f"Server: http://{args.host}:{args.port}")
   161	    print(f"Onboarding: http://{args.host}:{args.port}/onboarding")
   162	
   163	
   164	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None):
   165	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   166	        prog="haxjobs", description="Self-hosted job search platform"
   167	    )
   168	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")
   169	
   170	    start = sub.add_parser("start", help="Start the server")
   171	    start.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
   172	    start.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241)
   173	    start.add_argument("--no-browser", action="store_true", help="Don't open browser")
   174	    start.add_argument("--reload", action="store_true", help="Reload backend on code changes")
   175	    start.set_defaults(func=cmd_start)
   176	
   177	    agent = sub.add_parser("agent", help="Use the native HaxJobs agent")
   178	    agent_sub = agent.add_subparsers(dest="agent_command")
   179	    ask = agent_sub.add_parser("ask", help="Ask the agent a question")
   180	    ask.add_argument("prompt", nargs="*", help="Prompt text; stdin is used if omitted")
   181	    ask.add_argument("--system", default=None, help="Extra system/context text")
   182	    ask.add_argument("--plain", action="store_true", help="Skip HaxJobs identity prompt")
   183	    ask.add_argument("--tools", help="Comma-separated tools: web_search,fetch_page,db_query")
   184	    ask.add_argument("--max-turns", type=int, default=5)
   185	    ask.set_defaults(func=cmd_agent_ask)
   186	
   187	    dev = sub.add_parser("dev", help="Development utilities")
   188	    dev.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1", help="Server host")
   189	    dev.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241, help="Server port")
   190	    dev_sub = dev.add_subparsers(dest="dev_command")
   191	
   192	    reset_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("reset", help="Reset onboarding state (keeps provider config)")
   193	    reset_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_reset)
   194	
   195	    status_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("status", help="Show onboarding state")
   196	    status_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_status)
   197	
   198	    restart_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("restart", help="Kill current server, reset state, start fresh")
   199	    restart_cmd.add_argument("--project-root", default=None, help="Project directory")
   200	    restart_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_restart)
   201	
   202	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   203	    if not hasattr(args, "func"):
   204	        parser.print_help()
   205	        sys.exit(0)
   206	    args.func(args)
   207	
   208	
   209	if __name__ == "__main__":
   210	    main()

content.text:
1	[project]
     2	name = "haxjobs"
     3	version = "1.0.0.dev0"
     4	description = "Self-hosted job search platform"
     5	requires-python = ">=3.12"
     6	license = {text = "MIT"}
     7	authors = [{name = "Arinze Elenasulu"}]
     8	readme = "README.md"
     9	dependencies = [
    10	    "fastapi>=0.115",
    11	    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.34",
    12	    "markdown>=3.10.2",
    13	    "openai>=2.44.0",
    14	    "python-multipart>=0.0.32",
    15	]
    16	
    17	[project.scripts]
    18	haxjobs = "haxjobs.cli:main"
    19	
    20	[build-system]
    21	requires = ["hatchling"]
    22	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
    23	
    24	[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
    25	packages = ["src/haxjobs"]
    26	
    27	[dependency-groups]
    28	dev = [
    29	    "pytest>=9.1.1",
    30	]

--- cli ---
     1	"""HaxJobs CLI."""
     2	import argparse
     3	import sys
     4	
     5	
     6	def _csv(value: str | None) -> list[str] | None:
     7	    if not value:
     8	        return None
     9	    return [item.strip() for item in value.split(",") if item.strip()]
    10	
    11	
    12	def cmd_start(args):
    13	    """Start the HaxJobs server."""
    14	    from haxjobs.server.main import run
    15	    run(host=args.host, port=args.port, open_browser=not args.no_browser, reload=args.reload)
    16	
    17	
    18	def cmd_agent_ask(args):
    19	    """Ask the native HaxJobs agent a question from the terminal."""
    20	    from haxjobs.agent import Agent, build_system_prompt, load_identity
    21	
    22	    prompt = " ".join(args.prompt).strip()
    23	    if not prompt and not sys.stdin.isatty():
    24	        prompt = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    25	    if not prompt:
    26	        raise SystemExit("Provide a prompt or pipe one on stdin.")
    27	
    28	    system = args.system
    29	    if not args.plain:
    30	        system = build_system_prompt(load_identity(), context_files=system or "")
    31	
    32	    tools = _csv(args.tools)
    33	    if tools:
    34	        text = Agent(tools=tools).run_with_tools(
    35	            prompt,
    36	            system=system,
    37	            max_turns=args.max_turns,
    38	        )
    39	    else:
    40	        text = Agent().run(prompt, system=system)
    41	    print(text)
    42	
    43	
    44	def cmd_dev_reset(args):
    45	    """Reset onboarding state for development — keeps provider config."""
    46	    from pathlib import Path
    47	    from urllib import request
    48	    from haxjobs.config import STATE_DIR
    49	
    50	    # Clear project runtime state
    51	    removed = []
    52	    if STATE_DIR.exists():
    53	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
    54	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
    55	                continue  # ponytail: preserve DB, just clear profile
    56	            if p.is_file():
    57	                p.unlink()
    58	            elif p.is_dir():
    59	                import shutil
    60	                shutil.rmtree(p)
    61	            removed.append(p.name)
    62	    if removed:
    63	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
    64	    else:
    65	        print("State: nothing to clear")
    66	
    67	    # Also clear in-memory session if server is running
    68	    try:
    69	        req = request.Request(
    70	            f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/reset",
    71	            method="POST",
    72	        )
    73	        request.urlopen(req, timeout=2)
    74	        print("Server session: cleared")
    75	    except Exception:
    76	        try:
    77	            request.urlopen(
    78	                f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/reset",
    79	                timeout=2,
    80	            )
    81	            print("Server session: cleared")
    82	        except Exception:
    83	            print("Server session: not running (run: haxjobs dev restart)")
    84	
    85	
    86	def cmd_dev_status(args):
    87	    """Show current onboarding state."""
    88	    from pathlib import Path
    89	    from haxjobs.features.onboarding.service import load_profile
    90	
    91	    print(f"Profile file : {'exists' if load_profile() else 'none'}")
    92	
    93	    try:
    94	        from urllib import request
    95	        import json
    96	        req = request.Request(f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/onboarding/status")
    97	        resp = request.urlopen(req, timeout=2)
    98	        data = json.loads(resp.read())
    99	        print(f"Server status: {data.get('stage', 'unknown')}")
   100	
   101	        resp = request.urlopen(f"http://{args.host}:{args.port}/api/discovery/status", timeout=2)
   102	        discovery = json.loads(resp.read())
   103	        connected = isinstance(discovery.get("running"), bool) and isinstance(discovery.get("scrapers"), list)
   104	        print(f"Discovery API: {'connected' if connected else 'stale'}")
   105	    except Exception:
   106	        print("Server status: not running")
   107	
   108	
   109	def cmd_dev_restart(args):
   110	    """Kill running dev server and start a fresh one with reload."""
   111	    import os, signal, subprocess, sys
   112	    from pathlib import Path
   113	
   114	    # Kill existing
   115	    killed = False
   116	    for line in os.popen(f"ss -tlnp 'sport = :{args.port}'").readlines():
   117	        if "pid=" in line:
   118	            import re
   119	            m = re.search(r"pid=(\d+)", line)
   120	            if m:
   121	                os.kill(int(m.group(1)), signal.SIGTERM)
   122	                killed = True
   123	                print(f"Killed pid {m.group(1)}")
   124	
   125	    # Reset filesystem state (skip API call since we just killed the server)
   126	    from haxjobs.config import STATE_DIR
   127	    removed = []
   128	    if STATE_DIR.exists():
   129	        for p in sorted(STATE_DIR.glob("*")):
   130	            if p.name == "haxjobs.db":
   131	                continue
   132	            if p.is_file():
   133	                p.unlink()
   134	            elif p.is_dir():
   135	                import shutil
   136	                shutil.rmtree(p)
   137	            removed.append(p.name)
   138	    if removed:
   139	        print(f"State reset: {', '.join(removed)}")
   140	
   141	    # Start fresh
   142	    print("Starting dev server…")
   143	    os.chdir(args.project_root or os.getcwd())
   144	    subprocess.Popen(
   145	        [
   146	            sys.executable,
   147	            "-m",
   148	            "haxjobs.server.main",
   149	            "--no-browser",
   150	            "--reload",
   151	            "--host",
   152	            args.host,
   153	            "--port",
   154	            str(args.port),
   155	        ],
   156	        env={**os.environ, "PYTHONPATH": "src:."},
   157	        stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   158	        stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   159	    )
   160	    print(f"Server: http://{args.host}:{args.port}")
   161	    print(f"Onboarding: http://{args.host}:{args.port}/onboarding")
   162	
   163	
   164	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None):
   165	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   166	        prog="haxjobs", description="Self-hosted job search platform"
   167	    )
   168	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command")
   169	
   170	    start = sub.add_parser("start", help="Start the server")
   171	    start.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
   172	    start.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241)
   173	    start.add_argument("--no-browser", action="store_true", help="Don't open browser")
   174	    start.add_argument("--reload", action="store_true", help="Reload backend on code changes")
   175	    start.set_defaults(func=cmd_start)
   176	
   177	    agent = sub.add_parser("agent", help="Use the native HaxJobs agent")
   178	    agent_sub = agent.add_subparsers(dest="agent_command")
   179	    ask = agent_sub.add_parser("ask", help="Ask the agent a question")
   180	    ask.add_argument("prompt", nargs="*", help="Prompt text; stdin is used if omitted")
   181	    ask.add_argument("--system", default=None, help="Extra system/context text")
   182	    ask.add_argument("--plain", action="store_true", help="Skip HaxJobs identity prompt")
   183	    ask.add_argument("--tools", help="Comma-separated tools: web_search,fetch_page,db_query")
   184	    ask.add_argument("--max-turns", type=int, default=5)
   185	    ask.set_defaults(func=cmd_agent_ask)
   186	
   187	    dev = sub.add_parser("dev", help="Development utilities")
   188	    dev.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1", help="Server host")
   189	    dev.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8241, help="Server port")
   190	    dev_sub = dev.add_subparsers(dest="dev_command")
   191	
   192	    reset_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("reset", help="Reset onboarding state (keeps provider config)")
   193	    reset_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_reset)
   194	
   195	    status_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("status", help="Show onboarding state")
   196	    status_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_status)
   197	
   198	    restart_cmd = dev_sub.add_parser("restart", help="Kill current server, reset state, start fresh")
   199	    restart_cmd.add_argument("--project-root", default=None, help="Project directory")
   200	    restart_cmd.set_defaults(func=cmd_dev_restart)
   201	
   202	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   203	    if not hasattr(args, "func"):
   204	        parser.print_help()
   205	        sys.exit(0)
   206	    args.func(args)
   207	
   208	
   209	if __name__ == "__main__":
   210	    main()
